title: Hierarchical Priors in Perceptual Uncertainty and Psychosis Proneness - A Random-Dot Kinematogram task with low- and high-level priors
description: This EEG-BIDS dataset contains cue-locked recordings from 43 neurotypical adults performing a random-dot kinematogram task designed to dissociate low-level and high-level priors during perceptual decision-making. The study investigates how hierarchical priors bias behavior and modulate occipital oscillatory and aperiodic EEG activity using signal detection theory and generalized drift-diffusion modeling. Three within-subject conditions (baseline, low-level prior, high-level prior) were tested with individualized motion coherence thresholds.
methods_description: Continuous EEG was recorded from 31 active Ag/AgCl electrodes (10/20 system) using an actiChamp Plus amplifier at 1000 Hz, referenced to FCz with ground at FPz. Stimuli were random-dot kinematograms (1200 white dots, 2.53 deg/s) presented on a 240 Hz LCD monitor. Individual motion sensitivity was estimated with QUEST staircases targeting 75% discrimination accuracy. The experiment used a within-subject design with three conditions: baseline (neutral 750 Hz tone), low-level prior (600/900 Hz tones predicting motion with 75% contingency), and high-level prior (cover story about tinted glasses). Each condition included learning and test blocks with 160 trials per condition in main analyses.
